title: Fine, electric mountain bikes don’t suck
summary: The author recounts their skepticism toward electric mountain bikes and how testing the Amflow PX Carbon Pro changed their perspective. The e-bike's powerful motor compensates for technique issues and makes steep climbs more enjoyable, challenging the purist bias against assisted biking.
content:
You don’t need a $10,000 Amflow PX Carbon Pro, but it doesn’t hurt. | Photo by Thomas Ricker / The Verge	

Cheater, I'd grumble between huffs as yet another e-bike rider casually skittered past me on a steep ascent. It's this purist attitude that, for years, has left me blind to one simple fact: electric mountain bikes are fun!
My attitude adjustment came a few weeks ago, the very first time I rode an Amflow PX Carbon Pro fitted with the incredibly compact, lightweight, and powerful M2S motor from Avinox, a new DJI offshoot that has incumbents like Bosch and Specialized on edge. The motor doesn't make me a speed demon, but it does let me suck better by compensating whenever my poor technique kills the momentum.
Mountain biking has a long histor …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 好吧，电动山地车并不差劲
zh.summary: 作者讲述了他对电动山地车的怀疑态度，以及测试Amflow PX Carbon Pro如何改变了他的看法。这款电动自行车强大的电机补偿了技术缺陷，使陡峭的上坡更加愉快，挑战了对辅助骑行的纯粹主义偏见。

title: Neil Rimer thinks the AI money is coming back out
summary: Index Ventures co-founder Neil Rimer predicts that the substantial wealth generated by AI in Silicon Valley will eventually be redistributed, either voluntarily or by force. His prediction reflects growing concerns about AI wealth concentration in the tech industry.
content: Neil Rimer, the venture capitalist who co-founded Index Ventures, predicts the historic wealth AI is generating in Silicon Valley will have to be redistributed, voluntarily or involuntarily.

zh.title: Neil Rimer认为AI产生的资金会流出
zh.summary: Index Ventures联合创始人Neil Rimer预测，硅谷AI产生的巨大财富最终将被重新分配，要么自愿要么被迫。他的预测反映了人们对科技行业AI财富集中的日益增长的担忧。
